codeReview.summary: Compared against the prior failed feature (fd4b7f0), this fix moves cipher usage into the relay transport layer by adding RelayClient.sendEncrypted/readDecrypted and validating them with relay-backed integration tests. The added tests exercise ciphertext-only wire/store behavior, recipient decrypt success, and tamper rejection in end-to-end relay send/read paths, which addresses the original 'cipher only tested in isolation' failure.

issues.description: Remote CLI send/read paths still call RelayClient.send/read directly (src/cli.ts:414, src/cli.ts:657), so encrypted transport is currently opt-in via sendEncrypted/readDecrypted rather than the default CLI remote flow.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The mors-global-worker skill requires manual checks for user-facing changes or an explicit handoff rationale when manual checks are not applicable, but this handoff only listed automated commands and still marked followedProcedure=true.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: .factory/skills/mors-global-worker/SKILL.md step 8 vs handoff verification block in mission handoff file 2026-02-28T14-12-03-593Z__global-async-fix-e2ee-transport-integration__f6fd3a52-d559-48d8-9f02-169c7da5808c.json

summary: Pass: the fix integrates E2EE operations into actual relay transport methods and adds relay-backed decrypt/tamper tests, resolving the prior isolation-only implementation gap. One non-blocking follow-up remains: CLI remote commands are not yet wired to the encrypted RelayClient methods.
